theory_nodes.concept: Cultural practitioner -- more than a rapper. A hip-hop cultural practitioner connected to 206 Zulu. Commitment to all foundational elements of hip-hop, not just MCing.
examples: 206 Zulu connection: MCing, DJing, b-boying, graffiti, knowledge.
The distinction: rapper makes songs, practitioner lives culture.
theory_nodes.description: Cultural practitioner. 206 Zulu. Hip-hop as lived culture.

genealogy.relationship: Kung Foo Grip at 164. Cultural practitioner.

theory_nodes.concept: Battle-tested delivery -- cypher-refined precision. Multi-syllabic rhyme schemes. Competitive intensity. Delivery born in live confrontation.
examples: Live performance energy captured in recordings.

genealogy.relationship: Kung Foo Grip at 164. Battle-tested delivery.

theory_nodes.concept: 206 Zulu orbit -- 22 degrees after the collective entry (142). The gravitational pull of the cultural bedrock. Artists connected to 206 Zulu recur through the hip-hop era.
examples: Kung Foo Grip at 164 (22 degrees later).
Cultural incubation produces artists across decades.

genealogy.relationship: Kung Foo Grip at 164. 206 Zulu returns.

cross_references.relationship: COLLECTIVE RETURN! 22 degrees later. Cultural bedrock produces another artist. The gravitational pull.
PRODUCTION + PERFORMANCE! Boom Bap: production identity. Kung Foo Grip: performance identity. Two kinds of cultural commitment.
LABEL CONTRAST! Porter Ray: Sub Pop (institutional). Kung Foo Grip: 206 Zulu (communal). Different support structures.
